posts.text: Dropping the guards didn’t make me better. It made it real. Watch Guards Down: https://youtu.be/M8RF-v1P4QY
drafts: Dropping the guards didn’t make me better. It made it real. Watch Guards Down: https://youtu.be/M8RF-v1P4QY
Left felt natural. Right felt possible. Neither felt settled. Guards Down: https://youtu.be/M8RF-v1P4QY
Guards Down came from the moment protection stopped making the choice for me. https://youtu.be/M8RF-v1P4QY

posts.text: Older song, current mood: Brain Rot. Full signal at lilyroo.com.
drafts: Older song, current mood: Brain Rot. Full signal at lilyroo.com.
Brain Rot was a joke until it started sounding like a diary. More archive at lilyroo.com.
Some songs stay current because the weather inside your head never really clears. Link in bio: lilyroo.com.

posts.text: Guards Down came from bowling with Da, trying to choose between left and right while the bumpers were finally down. Full story and archive at lilyroo.com.
drafts: Guards Down came from bowling with Da, trying to choose between left and right while the bumpers were finally down. Full story and archive at lilyroo.com.
This song started at a bowling lane: no bumpers, no clean answer, just trying to decide which hand was really mine. More at lilyroo.com.
Da was already talking scholarships. I was still trying to figure out left or right. That gap became Guards Down. Archive: lilyroo.com.

posts.text: Too many tabs. One nervous system. Full song on YouTube @lilyroo.artist #BrainRot
drafts: Too many tabs. One nervous system. Full song on YouTube @lilyroo.artist #BrainRot
Brain Rot but make it folk punk. Full signal on YouTube. #BrainRot
Late again. Right on emotional schedule. Watch the full transmission on YouTube. #BrainRot

posts.text: If you can’t read clocks, maybe time is just a myth. Subscribe on YouTube if you’re into this signal.

Clocks measure numbers, not grief. Full signal on the channel.
